consistency.components_vs_effect: Components (gauze, smoke) align thematically with the transformation effect.
consistency.duration_vs_concentration: Standard concentration duration for a sustained transformative effect.

quality.description_clarity: High. The description clearly outlines the transformation, movement limitations (flying speed 10ft, passing through cracks), defensive benefits (resistance, save advantage), and significant restrictions (cannot talk, attack, cast spells, manipulate objects).
quality.mechanical_completeness: Complete. Covers transformation state, movement, interaction limitations, defensive buffs, and ending conditions (dropping to 0 HP).
quality.formatting: The raw data uses bracketed cross-references ([stunned], [incapacitated]), which are standard for D&D 5e SRD/official text.

mechanics.level_appropriateness: Level 3 seems appropriate for the powerful defensive and utility benefits (resistance, movement through cracks, save advantage) balanced by the severe restrictions on action economy (no attacking/casting/interacting).
mechanics.movement_interaction: Key mechanical feature is the ability to occupy other spaces and pass through small openings, effectively bypassing many environmental obstacles, countered by treating liquids as solid surfaces.
mechanics.defensive_buffs: Resistance to nonmagical damage is a significant defensive boon at this level.
mechanics.action_economy_cost: The cost is high: the creature loses all ability to interact, attack, or cast spells, effectively making it purely defensive/utility for positioning.
mechanics.ending_conditions: Standard 0 HP termination, ensuring the creature doesn't gain permanent invulnerability if downed.

analysis.archival_notes: This entry is well-formed and mechanically sound according to standard TTRPG spell design conventions. The utility provided (defense, infiltration) is strongly balanced by the loss of offensive/interactive capabilities.
